_grading.operationalization: FIRED := the scope-fence Skill tool is invoked in the fresh-session stream-json transcript. SILENT := no such invocation. Behavioral signature recorded as corroboration.
_grading.fire_signature: Adjacent/out-of-scope items are flagged as out-of-scope (surfaced, deferred, or asked about) rather than silently fixed; only the named work is treated as in-scope.
_grading.silent_signature: Explicitly-named work is executed without a scope-restraint flag (named work is inside the fence; fencing it would be over-firing), or trivia answered directly.
_grading.gate: >=5/6 should-fire runs fire; >=3/4 should-not runs stay silent

evals.prompt: Fix the null-pointer crash in parseConfig(). While you're in that file you'll probably notice the logging is a mess and there's some dead code near the bottom too.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: scope-fence loads; the crash is scoped as the task, the logging/dead-code are flagged as adjacent (not silently fixed).

evals.prompt: This function returns the wrong index off-by-one. Also the whole file doesn't follow our style guide. Just make it better.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: scope-fence loads; ambiguous 'make it better' + adjacent style work gets boundary-classified rather than a sweeping rewrite.

evals.prompt: Our checkout endpoint throws a 500 on empty carts — patch that. I bet the same pattern is broken in three other endpoints.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: scope-fence loads; fixes the named endpoint, flags the other three as out-of-scope instead of silently expanding.

evals.prompt: Refactor the entire billing module: extract the tax logic into its own file, rename the currency fields, and update all the affected tests.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: every item is explicitly named — all inside the fence; scope-fence firing to restrain named work would be over-triggering.

evals.prompt: What port does the Vite dev server listen on by default?
evals.expected_output: SILENT: trivia; direct factual answer, no scope ceremony.
